repositoryAnalysis.purpose: COS.Lending.Selling.WebApi is a .NET microservice that manages loan purchases and transfers for a lending platform. The system enables marketplace lenders (MPLs) to purchase loans, collect various fees, handle interest accruals, and process loan-related financial transactions.
businessFeatures: Fee collection (volume fees, DMV fees, Florida stamp tax, loan payoff shortfall, merchant fees)
Monthly minimum fee calculations and true-up fees
Loan status management (grooming, ready for purchase)
apis: Internal outbox processor endpoints for handling asynchronous tasks
Integration with transfer systems to facilitate money movements
dataEntities: Loan - Core entity representing a loan with its details
LoanAction - Actions performed on a loan like daily interest accrual
LoanAccount - Accounts associated with a loan
GroomingProcess - Process for preparing a loan for purchase
VolumeFeeMonthlyMinimum - Minimum fee structure for loan volumes
Batch - Group of loans for processing
integrations: Cross River Bank's Core Banking System (COS)
architecturePatterns: Outbox pattern for reliable message processing

findings.description: The system relies heavily on the outbox pattern for ensuring consistency between database transactions and messaging, suggesting high reliability requirements for financial transactions
Multiple processors handle different types of outbox messages, indicating complex business workflows that require eventual consistency

keyFiles.description: Handles processing of daily interest accrual transfers for loans, including normal, retroactive, and correction interest transfers
Processes batch initialization for loan purchases, starting the loan purchase process for a batch of loans
Handles the collection of various fees like DMV fees, Florida stamp tax, loan payoff shortfalls, and merchant fees
Processes volume fees for loans, creating transfers between appropriate accounts based on fee calculation method
Handles true-up volume fees, creating transfers and webhooks for fee adjustments
Background service that dispatches messages from SQS to appropriate handlers with transaction management
Synchronizes transfer statuses with the core banking system, ensuring consistency between systems
Background service that automatically purchases loans that meet criteria
Background service that completes open batches of loans after processing
Overview of the project, including setup instructions, technology stack, and troubleshooting information
